commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: In their ministry of the forgiveness of sin, pastors do not exercise the right of some independent power. For not in their own name but in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit do they forgive sins. They ask, the Godhead forgives. The service is enabled by humans, but the gift comes from the Power on high. .

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: How can sins be rightly remitted unless the very One against whom one has sinned grants the pardon? Against Heresies.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: They persecuted Jesus not only because he broke the Sabbath but also because he said that God was his Father, making himself equal with God, which is a far more drastic declaration. He confirmed this through his own actions…. The scribes themselves had devised this definition. They themselves had introduced the precept. They themselves had interpreted the law. But he proceeded to entangle them in their own words. In effect he said: It is you yourselves who have confessed that forgiveness of sins is given to God alone.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Who, moreover, was able to forgive sins? This is His alone prerogative: for "who remitteth sins but God alone? "

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
